title: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) — implicit volumetric scene representation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in Mildenhall et al. 2020 NeRF and canonical volume-rendering literature (Kajiya 1986, Max 1995).

E.description: NeRF represents a scene as an MLP F_theta: (x, y, z, theta, phi) -> (sigma, c) mapping a 3D position and view direction to volumetric density and RGB color. Rendering a pixel casts a ray and numerically integrates the classical volume-rendering equation along quadrature samples; training fits theta by back-propagating photometric error on K posed reference images.
E.forward_model: C_hat(r) = sum_{i=1}^{N_samp} T_i * (1 - exp(-sigma_i * delta_i)) * c_i   with   T_i = exp(-sum_{j<i} sigma_j * delta_j)
E.world_state_x: Continuous volumetric field (sigma(x), c(x, d)) parameterised by MLP weights theta
E.observation_y: K posed reference images {I_k, T_k}_{k=1..K} with per-frame extrinsics T_k and intrinsics K_intr
physical_parameters_theta: Per-view camera extrinsics {T_k} (SE(3)) and intrinsics K_intr (pinhole)
Number of samples per ray N_samp (hierarchical coarse/fine)
Scene bounding box and near/far planes

W.regime: Strongly non-convex MLP fitting. Unique 3D scene only when K views cover the scene with sufficient baseline and pose accuracy; view-dependent effects (specularity, transparency) can be absorbed into radiance ambiguously. Pose error, lighting drift, and under-covered regions cause floaters, baked-in artifacts, and depth collapse.

C.solver_class: SGD / Adam on MLP weights with hierarchical sampling; accelerated variants (Instant-NGP hashgrid, TensoRF factorisation, Plenoxels voxel grid)
C.error_bound: |C_hat - C|_L2 <= C1 * sigma_pixel + C2 * pose_error_rad + C3 * under_view_coverage + C4 * view_dependent_residual
C.complexity: O(N_rays * N_samp * F_theta_cost); Instant-NGP reduces F_theta_cost by ~100x via hash grid
